label: Cardiomegaly
report_text:
CHEST COMPUTED TOMOGRAPHY SCAN REPORT
Clinical information
None.
Technique
5mm thick axial images were obtained through the chest before and after the administration of intravenous contrast. 1.25mm thick axial images were obtained as coronal and sagittal reformatted images.
Comparison
No previous exams available.
Comments
There are multiple cavitary lesions of varying sizes which contain intracavitary fairly rounded enhancing soft tissue density masses with air crescent sign seen predominantly in both upper lobes in keeping with  fungus balls, the largest of which measures about 7.4 x4.4 cm in dimension , located in the left apex. There is associated diffuse pulmonary emphysematous changes bilaterally and apical pleural thickening. No adjacent bony destruction noted.  No effusion seen.
The heart size is normal without pericardial effusion.  The thoracic aorta demonstrates no evidence of focal aneurysmal dilatation or dissection. The pulmonary arteries are normal in caliber without evidence of intraluminal filling defect.
The visualized portions of the upper abdomen are within normal limits.
No pathologic lymphadenopathy is identified.
Soft tissue and osseous structures are unremarkable.
Impression
Aspergillomas with background pulmonary bullous emphysema.
Radiologists

The costophrenic sulci and hemidiaphragms are preserved.
The bony rib thorax is within normal limits. No lytic or sclerotic lesions seen.
Extrapulmonary soft tissues are normal.
Impression:
Multiple pulmonary nodules likely metastatic lung disease.

Widespread inhomogenous opacities over both lung fields (left more than right )
A  Large air fluid level over the right lower lung zone   With a pencil thin line opacity over the lateral/peripheral region noted 
The cardiac shadow is normal in size 
The cardiothoracic ratio  is 49.5%
The diaphragm, left costophrenic angle and bony thorax are within normal 
Soft tissue emphysema noted  in the axillae and supraclavicular regions
Dressing artifact is noted over the left lateral chest wall.
Tubal artifact is noted in the right lung field, the tip is noted at the 2nd right intercostal space. Another tubal artifact is noted across the right lung field suggestive of oxygen delivery tubing.
Conclusion:
Features suggest Acute respiratory distress syndrome with right sided hydro pneumothorax and subcutaneous emphysema

Surgical clips projected over the left thoracic soft tissue

Left hydropneumothorax with pneumonic consolidation and atelectasis

CHEST RADIOGRAPH REPORT
There is a homogenous opacity with associated lamellar tracking observed in the right middle and lower lung zones, silhouetting the right cardiac border and ipsilateral hemidiaphragm.
A chest tube artefact is observed within the right hemithorax entering through the lateral 7th intercostal space with Its tip seen to lie in the 11th intercostal space medially. Adjacent soft tissue emphysema is noted.

There is blunting of the left costophrenic sulcus.
The heart size is indeterminate, however there is mitralisation of the left heart border.
The bony rib cage and peripheral soft tissues are normal.
CONCLUSION
Overall appearances are those of cardiac decompensation with bilateral pulmonary edema and moderate right pleural effusion

There is diffuse reticular opacification of both lung fields with associated peritracheal and peribronchial cuffing.

Fracture of the neck of the right humerus with medial displacement of the humeral shaft is noted. No callus formation is seen.
The peripheral soft tissues are normal.
FINDINGS

label: Degenerative / Structural Changes
report_text:
CHEST RADIOGRAPH REPORT
Indication: Hypertensive heart disease
Findings:
 • The image is mildly rotated to the right.
 • The heart is enlarged, with a cardiothoracic ratio of approximately 53.5%.
 • The aorta is unfolded, consistent with age-related or hypertensive changes.
 • The mediastinum, hila, and pulmonary vasculature appear within normal limits.
 • No focal lung lesions or areas of consolidation are identified.
 • The costophrenic angles and hemidiaphragms are preserved.
 • The bony thoracic cage and visualized soft tissues are unremarkable.
Conclusion:
 • Cardiomegaly with unfolded aorta, likely related to hypertensive heart disease.
 • No acute pulmonary or pleural pathology.
